samples.xml_description:
<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>Asynchronous FIFO implementation with configurable parameters WIDTH and DEPTH.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock signal.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset signal.</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset signal.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment signal.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment signal.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data input.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full signal.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty signal.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WIDTH" value="8">FIFO data width.</parameter>
        <parameter id="DEPTH" value="16">FIFO depth.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        // Define internal signals
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] waddr;
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] raddr;
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] wptr;
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] rptr;
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] wptr_buff;
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] rptr_buff;
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] waddr_bin;
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] raddr_bin;
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] rptr_syn;
        // Convert binary to Gray code
        function [$clog2(DEPTH):0] bin_to_gray(input [$clog2(DEPTH):0] bin);
            begin
                bin_to_gray = bin ^ (bin >> 1);
            end
        endfunction
        // Convert Gray code to binary
        function [$clog2(DEPTH):0] gray_to_bin(input [$clog2(DEPTH):0] gray);
            integer i;
            begin
                gray_to_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)] = gray[$clog2(DEPTH)];
                for(i = $clog2(DEPTH)-1; i >= 0; i=i-1) begin
                    gray_to_bin[i] = gray_to_bin[i+1] ^ gray[i];
                end
            end
        endfunction
        // Update write pointer
        always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
            if(~wrstn) begin
                waddr <= 0;
                wptr <= 0;
            end else begin
                if(winc && !wfull) begin
                    waddr <= waddr + 1;
                    wptr <= bin_to_gray(waddr + 1);
                end
            end
        end
        // Update read pointer
        always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
            if(~rrstn) begin
                raddr <= 0;
                rptr <= 0;
            end else begin
                if(rinc && !rempty) begin
                    raddr <= raddr + 1;
                    rptr <= bin_to_gray(raddr + 1);
                end
            end
        end
        // Synchronize read pointer to write clock domain
        always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
            if(~wrstn) begin
                rptr_syn <= 0;
                rptr_buff <= 0;
            end else begin
                rptr_syn <= rptr;
                rptr_buff <= rptr_syn;
            end
        end
        // Synchronize write pointer to read clock domain
        always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
            if(~rrstn) begin
                wptr_buff <= 0;
            end else begin
                wptr_buff <= wptr;
            end
        end
        // Assign output signals
        assign wfull = (wptr == {~rptr_buff[$clog2(DEPTH)], rptr_buff[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]});
        assign rempty = (rptr == wptr_buff);
        assign rdata = raddr_bin;
        // Connect input signals to dual-port RAM module
        assign wen = winc & !wfull;
        assign ren = rinc & !rempty;
        assign wdata = wdata;
        assign rdata = rdata;
        
        // Dual-port RAM instance
        dual_port_RAM #(WIDTH, DEPTH) ram_inst(
            .wclk(wclk),
            .rclk(rclk),
            .wen(wen),
            .ren(ren),
            .waddr(waddr),
            .raddr(raddr),
            .wdata(wdata),
            .rdata(rdata)
        );
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Implements an asynchronous FIFO using dual-port RAM, write and read controllers, and pointer synchronizers. Uses Gray code for read and write pointers. Implements full and empty flags.</implementation>
</module>
